metric_definitions.enforcement_counts.https_unreachable: Number of origins for which neither the https_connectivity module nor the HSTS module reported a successful HTTPS response (https_connectivity.success is False and HSTS.https_ok is False or missing). These are effectively HTTPS-unreachable for our scanner.
metric_definitions.enforcement_counts.no_redirect: Number of origins where the HSTS HTTP probe recorded a non-3xx status code for http://origin/ (redirect_status not in the 300–399 range), meaning that HTTP was served without an HTTP->HTTPS redirect.
metric_definitions.enforcement_counts.redirect_to_https: Number of origins where the HSTS HTTP probe recorded a 3xx status code and redirected_to_https=True, meaning that http://origin/ redirected directly to an HTTPS URL.
metric_definitions.enforcement_counts.redirect_to_https_hst: Number of origins where http://origin/ redirected to HTTPS and the HTTPS response included 'strong HSTS' (has_hsts=True, max-age >= 1 year, includeSubDomains=True).

Number of origins where http://origin/ redirected to HTTPS and the HTTPS response included HSTS (has_hsts=True), but the configuration did not meet the 'strong HSTS' definition (i.e., max-age < 1 year or includeSubDomains=False).
metric_definitions.enforcement_counts.redirect_to_https_no_: Number of origins where http://origin/ redirected to HTTPS (redirected_to_https=True), the HSTS module marked https_ok=True, but the HTTPS response did not include any Strict-Transport-Security header (has_hsts=False).
metric_definitions.hsts.fractions.pct_has_hsts_among_https_: Percentage of HTTPS-working origins (n_https_ok) that set any HSTS header.
metric_definitions.hsts.fractions.pct_hsts_strong_among_hst: Percentage of HSTS-enabled origins that meet the 'strong HSTS' criteria (max-age >= 1 year and includeSubDomains).
metric_definitions.hsts.fractions.pct_include_subdomains_am: Percentage of HSTS-enabled origins whose HSTS header includes the includeSubDomains directive.
metric_definitions.hsts.fractions.pct_maxage_1yr_among_hsts: Percentage of HSTS-enabled origins that configured max-age >= 1 year.
metric_definitions.hsts.fractions.pct_preload_among_hsts: Percentage of HSTS-enabled origins whose HSTS header contains the preload directive.
metric_definitions.hsts.n_has_hsts: Number of origins with https_ok=True whose HTTPS response included a Strict-Transport-Security header.
metric_definitions.hsts.n_hsts_include_subdomains: Number of HSTS-enabled origins (has_hsts=True) whose HSTS header included the includeSubDomains directive.
metric_definitions.hsts.n_hsts_maxage_1yr: Number of HSTS-enabled origins (has_hsts=True) where the parsed max-age directive was at least 31,536,000 seconds (one year).
metric_definitions.hsts.n_hsts_preload_flag: Number of HSTS-enabled origins (has_hsts=True) whose HSTS header included the preload directive. Note that this only checks the header flag, not actual presence on the browser preload list.
metric_definitions.hsts.n_hsts_strong: Number of HSTS-enabled origins (has_hsts=True) that simultaneously configured max-age >= 1 year and includeSubDomains=True (our definition of 'strong HSTS').
metric_definitions.hsts.n_https_ok: Number of origins where the HSTS module successfully completed the follow-up HTTPS request and recorded https_ok=True (final status in the 200–599 range).
metric_definitions.http_to_https.n_http_no_redirect: Number of origins where the HTTP probe succeeded but the status code was not in the 3xx range, meaning the site served a non-redirect response over HTTP.
metric_definitions.http_to_https.n_http_probe_error: Number of origins where the HSTS module did not record any HTTP status code for the HTTP probe (redirect_status is None), typically because the origin was offline or the HTTP request errored or timed out before a response.
metric_definitions.http_to_https.n_http_probe_ok: Number of origins for which the HSTS module recorded an HTTP status code when probing http://origin/ (i.e., the HTTP probe returned some response).
metric_definitions.http_to_https.n_http_redirect_not_to_htt: Number of origins where the HTTP probe status was 3xx but redirected_to_https=False, indicating a redirect that did not target HTTPS.
metric_definitions.http_to_https.n_http_redirect_to_https: Number of origins where the HTTP probe status was 3xx and the HSTS module marked redirected_to_https=True (Location header pointed to an HTTPS URL).
metric_definitions.http_to_https.redirect_code_counts: Histogram of concrete 3xx status codes (e.g., 301, 302, 307, 308) returned by the HTTP probe when redirect_status was in the 3xx range.
metric_definitions.https_connectivity.n_https_error: Number of origins where HTTPS was attempted but the https_connectivity row contains a non-empty error string (timeout, client error, etc.) and success=False.
metric_definitions.https_connectivity.n_https_non_https_fin: Number of origins where HTTPS was attempted and no explicit error was recorded, but the final_scheme reported by https_connectivity was not 'https'.
metric_definitions.https_connectivity.n_https_success: Number of origins where the https_connectivity module marked success=True and the final_scheme was HTTPS.
metric_definitions.https_connectivity.n_origins: Number of origins that appeared in the https_connectivity module results.
metric_definitions.https_connectivity.redirects_stats_succe: Descriptive statistics (count, mean, median, 95th percentile, max) of the number of redirects followed on the HTTPS path, computed only for origins where https_connectivity.success is True.
metric_definitions.https_connectivity.status_family_counts_: For origins with HTTPS success, counts of HTTP status families (2xx, 3xx, 4xx, 5xx) based on the final status code in the https_connectivity results.
